claim: Agentic engineering means knowing your system well enough not to babysit agents; vibe coding means not knowing and not looking
actionable: Build systems where you understand what agents will do, validate outputs, and iterate; stop assuming agents will figure it out on their own.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Blueprint engines combine deterministic code with agent loops to maximize leverage (code + agents beats either alone)
actionable: Interleave agent reasoning (for complex tasks) with deterministic code (linting, testing, git ops); don't make agents do everything.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: In-loop agentic coding (human supervises back-and-forth) vs out-loop (agents run unattended); shift work to out-loop for scale
actionable: Build out-loop systems where agents work unsupervised on isolated sandboxes; spend your time planning and reviewing, not iterating mid-task.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Give agents isolated dev environments (EC2, containers) that mirror your developer setup, not just Git worktrees
actionable: Provision full development environments for agents rather than trying to use worktrees; this enables parallelization and prevents cross-contamination.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Use rule files with conditional glob patterns to scope context to specific directories automatically
actionable: Create rules files with glob patterns that load context only when agents access specific subdirectories, reducing token bloat.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Build a toolshed (centralized MCP server) to manage 500+ tools and avoid token explosion from tool loading
actionable: Create a meta-MCP tool that agents can query to discover and select the right tool, rather than loading all tools at once.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Specialization (custom agents, custom harness, custom prompts) beats off-the-shelf tools for large codebases
actionable: If your codebase has >10k files or custom tech stack, fork/customize your agent instead of relying on generic Claude Code or Cursor.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Limit CI feedback loops for agents conservatively to manage cost, but this may reduce learning signal for agents
actionable: If you restrict agent iterations (e.g., 2 attempts max), be aware you're trading cost savings for reduced feedback signal; monitor agent learning impact.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Agents operating at scale should aim for zero-touch engineering (prompt to production) not just prompt to PR review
actionable: Design your agentic systems to ship directly to production for low-risk tasks; human review should be the exception, not the rule.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead

claim: Parallelization is the key leverage point: engineer time is the bottleneck, agents are parallelizable
actionable: Build systems that allow one engineer to supervise multiple agent sandboxes in parallel; this multiplies impact without adding headcount.
source_title: I Studied Stripe's AI Agents... Vibe Coding Is Already Dead
